mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 6 months signals ongoing monetary tightening that raises the discount rate on future equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as valuation multiples compress.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 60-day forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are positive in more than 55% of rising-rate months.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an inflection into labor-market deterioration that historically precedes equity drawdowns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical instances where the trigger fires, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive, or the signal's hit rate is not statistically distinguishable from the unconditional base rate (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising FEDFUNDS combined with decelerating RSAFS growth jointly indicate that tighter monetary policy is beginning to suppress consumer spending, a combination that predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days more reliably than either series alone because it confirms transmission of policy into real demand.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change > +0.5pp AND RSAFS 3-month annualized growth < 2%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with slowing consumption
failure_condition: The joint-trigger signal underperforms a single-series FEDFUNDS-only model in out-of-sample Rank IC, or fails to produce negative median forward returns in at least 55% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO during a monetary tightening regime signals contracting industrial output consistent with an approaching recession, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations reprice downward.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive real rates
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following INDPRO contraction during tightening regimes are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Continued month-over-month growth in PAYEMS signals resilient labor demand and household income support, which sustains near-term consumer confidence and produces modestly positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and 5-day forward returns stays below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds 6%, it signals that the Federal Reserve is likely to maintain or accelerate restrictive policy, raising rate-hike expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days via higher discount rates.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of months where CPI YoY exceeds 6%, or the effect disappears once controlling for FEDFUNDS trend.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in HOUST and rising FEDFUNDS indicates that higher borrowing costs are suppressing housing starts, an early and durable signal of broader demand contraction that predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the effect propagates through construction, durable goods, and employment channels.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing-sector stress
failure_condition: Joint HOUST-FEDFUNDS trigger fails to outperform a HOUST-only single-series model in Rank IC, or 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During a regime of simultaneously elevated UNRATE and flat-to-declining INDPRO, indicative of stagflation-like conditions, near-term equity markets exhibit heightened volatility and negative drift over the next 5 trading days as investors price in weak growth without policy relief.
regime_hint: stagflation-like regime with weak output and rising unemployment
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns during periods meeting the joint UNRATE/INDPRO condition are not significantly different from unconditional 5-day returns (p > 0.10).
